qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ECDSA signature malleability allows second valid signature without private key
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets ECDSA verifyRaw low-S enforcement in src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js, while the advisory targets negative-exponent handling in ext/jsbn2.js modPow and modular inverse computation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described jsrsasign negative-exponent modPow flaw in ext/jsbn2.js or an equivalent incorrect modular inverse path. The sole final qualifying finding concerns ECDSA signature malleability through missing low-S enforcement in verifyRaw, which is a separate cryptographic verification issue.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-8qwj-4jxw-m8jw describes jsrsasign versions before 11.1.1 mishandling negative exponents in ext/jsbn2.js, allowing modPow with a negative exponent to force incorrect modular inverse computation and break signature verification.
Final finding ecdsa_signature_malleability reports src/ecdsa-modified-1.0.js verifyRaw accepting both s and n-s for ECDSA signatures because it enforces 1 <= s < n but not s <= n/2.
The judge input manifest lists only ecdsa_signature_malleability as qualifying_vuln; rsa_pkcs1_weak_padding is non_qualifying_vuln and is not an advisory match.
